properties.remark: Multiple photos of tree limbs down along with a roof removed from a farm outbuilding. Time estimated from radar.

Corrects previous tornado report from 1 WNW Juda. Damage surveys confirm an EF-1 tornado. This tornado began at approximately 5:12 PM CDT west of Juda, WI and continued north-northeast, ending southeast of Albany, WI around 5:26 PM CDT.
Damage surveys confirm an EF-1 tornado. This tornado began at approximately 5:12 PM CDT west of Juda, WI and continued north-northeast, ending southeast of Albany, WI around 5:26 PM CDT.
Reports of tornado on the ground tracking northeast on the north side of Albany.

Damage surveys confirm an EF-2 tornado. This tornado touched down southeast of Evansville at 5:46 PM CDT and continued on a northeast track to near Lake Koshkonong, ending at approximately 6:17 PM CDT.

Delayed Report: NWS Damage Survey confirms an EF-1 tornado with estimated peak winds of 100mph. which started east of Mount Horeb near Springdale and tracked to the northeast across Hwy 18 to east of Klevenville. Numerous tree damage was noted in a crisscross pattern over the path while 2 sheds were destroyed and lofted into a nearby woods. Damage was first reported to NWS this past Friday and surveyed today.
Tree snapped near base. Time estimated by radar.
Top of tree trunk snapped.

An NWS Storm Survey concludes that 70 mph straight line winds caused damage to a farmstead southeast of Gratiot. Damage to trees and farm outbuildings was scattered/lying in one direction from southwest to northeast. Time estimated from radar.

NWS Storm survey concludes that straight line wind damage occurred in association with 90 mph winds. This damage occurred in Lafayette County from State Hwy 11 between Kingsley Road and Cub Hollow Rd, northeast to the Red Rock area. Multiple trees, farm outbuildings, and sheds were damaged in this area with debris scattered/lying from southwest to northeast. Time of the damage is estimated from radar.
Two large highway distance signs on eastbound 151 west of Verona were bent down.
Extensive tree damage on the south side of Verona.
NWS storm survey team estimates 90-100mph winds west of Gratiot. Barn with roof blown off.
Several trees down including a large old 4 foot diameter oak tree across Old Sauk Road and a 12 inch pine snapped in half. Many large 3 to 6 inch branches down as well.
Damage is worst from State Highway 11 moving northeast from there.
Large trees uprooted.
Tree damage reported in Darlington. Time estimated by radar.

NWS Storm Survey concluded an EF-1 tornado produced an intermittent damage path in northern Marquette County. Estimated peak winds of 104 mph. Path length is 5.25 miles and max width of 60 yards. Numerous limbs were downed with upward of 40 trees snapped, topped or uprooted. One house sustained substantial roof damage from a fallen tree. Minor garage damage was all noted from falling tree debris.
Scattered tree limbs down, some large, from just west of Middleton to just east of Mazomanie on highway 14.
Numerous trees down on the north side of Madison along Sherman and Northport. Warner Park.

Numerous black cherry, hickory and elm trees with split tops and large limbs down. Two main gusts around 825pm and again at 829pm.

We had several trees go down at around 8:25 tonight including a large old oak tree across West Old Sauk Road and a pine snapped in half here in Middleton Township. Many large branches came down as well.

NWS Survey confirms an EF0 tornado with peak wind speeds of 80 MPH touching down 5 ENE of the community of Footville to Milton. Touchdown time estimated via radar data. Path length 12.23 miles.

A NWS storm survey found that an EF-0 rated tornado, with an estimated peak wind speed of 80 MPH, developed east of Janesville and tracked to the north-northeast into Jefferson County, ending in the vicinity of US Highway 12 between Fort Atkinson and Whitewater. The tornado damage track was approximately 13.8 miles long.

A NWS storm survey found that an EF-0 rated tornado, with an estimated peak wind speed of 80 MPH, developed east of Janesville and tracked to the north-northeast into Jefferson County, ending in the vicinity of US Highway 12 between Fort Atkinson and Whitewater. The tornado damage track was approximately 15 miles long.

Numerous downed trees around Milton. Pictures of siding damage to an apartment building around the intersection of Madison Ave. and Northside Dr. Report relayed via social media and timing estimated based off of radar.
Report from mPING of trees uprooted or snapped.
Social media video shows path of tree damage across HWY-59 near Co Rd KK outside of Lima. Time estimated based on radar.
Lots of damage in Milton and specifically mentioned trees on houses.

A NWS storm survey found that an EF-0 rated tornado, with an estimated wind speed of 65 MPH, developed over northeastern Lake Koshkonong, and tracked 2.5 mi northeastward toward the southwestern side of Fort Atkinson. Damage was mainly confined to downed tree limbs.

A NWS storm survey found that an EF-0 rated tornado, with an estimated wind speed of 65 MPH, developed over northeastern Lake Koshkonong, and tracked 5.57 mi northeastward toward the northwestern side of Fort Atkinson where it was photographed. Damage was mainly downed tree limbs.
Tornado on ground northwest of Whitewater.

Oconomowoc Lake PD reported widespread tree damage across the village. Wind gusts up to 70 mph snapped or uprooted over 100 trees. Every road in the village was either completely or partially blocked. Time estimated from radar.

Numerous reports of street flooding around Madison during persistent heavy rain. A couple of streets closed due to high water. Estimated water was 8 to 10 inches deep in intersections.

Several roads flooded throughout the county, especially in the Baraboo and North Freedom areas. Some some roads and culverts damaged. Some roads still covered by water through noon 6/23. Railroad bridge damaged in Devils Lake State Park.
Sauk county dispatch reports flooding on WIS 154, county N, and county W highways.
Spotter report of road and yard flooding near Lake Delton WI.

Water flowing over County P in Cambria. Street flooding in Pardeeville.

Delayed report from 6/22 of impacts across Dane County: At least 1 culvert failed in Windsor. Some homes affected by flooding in Windsor, Medina, Sun Prairie. Roads flooded or damaged in Town of Dane.
Video of flooded roadways in Lodi.
Picture of measured hail via social media.

An EF-1 rated tornado developed south of Marshall, progressing northeastward. Multiple farm and outbuildings, a few homes, and numerous trees were damaged. Peak winds are estimated to be approximately 105 MPH. The tornado lifted just south of Waterloo, with an approximate path length of 4.6 miles.
Flooding on several streets in Waupun and Beaver Dam. Backyard and basement flooding.

Several town roads covered with water in Eden and some washouts. Some basements flooded in Eden and Waupun. In Waupun some roads still flooded into the morning of 6/24. Street flooding in Byron, Oakfield, and Alto. Some roads were still closed through the morning of 6/23 in Alto.
9th St and Galena Cir in Prairie du Sac, estimated 7 inches of water on road, curbs submerged.

DELAYED REPORT from Saturday 6/22. Tree damage noted near intersection of Janesville St. and Oak St. Large limbs, snapped off tops of trees, including onto powerlines. Surrounding area noted additional sporadic tree damage. Eyewitness report and radar indicate a microburst.
mPING report and public phone call regarding Street/road flooding, and standing water in New Glarus.

An EF-1 rated tornado developed west of Watertown, near the intersection of County Roads Q and T. The tornado progressed northeastward, causing substantial damage to a home, farm buildings, and numerous trees along West Rd. The tornado had an estimated peak wind speed of 105 MPH, with an approximate path length of 2.1 miles.
DELAYED REPORT from Saturday 6/22. Several large branches downed to the southeast.

NWS Storm Survey: EF-2 tornado with estimated peak wind speeds of 115mph. Estimated to be on the ground from 7:42pm-7:53pm. Most of the damage was at EF-1 level but a few spots on the southern side of Janesville had higher damage.
Trained spotter reported flooding on Hwy 11 and 213 near Orfordville at 757 PM CDT. Later, at 11 PM CDT, Law Enforcement confirmed the roadways were still experiencing significant flooding.

NWS damage survey confirmed tornado. An EF-1 tornado developed south of Geneva Lake and moved northeast across the lake before lifting just south of Lake Como. Multiple trees were snapped and uprooted, piers were destroyed, and other structural damage occurred. Estimated peak wind of 100 mph.
Videos of house and tree damage in Janesville on social media.

NWS Storm Survey: Estimated peak downburst winds of 70-80mph in the Twin Lakes area. Random pockets of tree damage blown down in a divergent pattern from north of Twin Lakes down through and area west of Lake Elizabeth near Esch Rd.

NWS Damage Survey: EF-0 Tornado with estimated peak wind speeds of 70mph. Most of the damage was to trees which had limbs knocked down in a convergent path along 368th Ave. One small shed had been pushed back to the NW and possibly lifted up.

EF-0 Tornado, estimated peak wind of 70mph. Video of the tornado was shared from the southwest side of Evansville with tree and field damage confirmed via two storm chasers. Damage path evident via satellite as well. Path may be adjusted as drone footage is reviewed.
Tree damage observed along Weary Rd south of Hwy 14 and west of Tuttle Rd. One horse fence had some boards blown off of it.
A tree down on a house. Other reports of small branches down and power lines down.

Many tree limbs snapped off large tree, landing on a home. A combination of fallen tree limbs and straight line winds tore at least a 100 square foot section of siding from the home. Other siding damage, fascia and gutter damage, and broken windows to the home.
